Card Description: Implement non-blocking background worker with job queue and lock file management for processing commits without blocking git operations. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 47-161.
Create JSONL-based job queue system with atomic append operations. Supports multiple job types (track-commit, sync, analyze). See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 992-1011.
Implement lock file system to prevent concurrent workers. Include stale lock detection via PID checking. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 123-141.
Add try-catch wrapping for all hook code with error logging to ~/.commitkit/errors.log. Never block commits on errors. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 642-677.
Implement error log with rotation (keep last 100 errors OR 7 days). Create `commitkit logs` command. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 1013-1023.
Reduce 5 separate git process spawns to 1 using format string with ASCII separators (\x1F). See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 614-640.
Implement 3-layer filtering system: environment variables → git notes → .commitkit-ignore file. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 445-482.

Parse YAML ignore file with support for: message_patterns, file_patterns, authors, branches, time_rules, size_rules, custom rules, diff_patterns, directory_patterns. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 354-411.
Support COMMITKIT_SKIP=1 and COMMITKIT_TRACK=1 environment variables for per-commit control. Highest priority in decision logic. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 335-341.
Read git notes for "commitkit:skip" and "commitkit:track" markers. Second priority in decision logic after env vars. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 343-350.
During `commitkit init`, ask user to choose tracking mode: opt-out (track all) vs opt-in (track only marked). Store in .commitkit/config. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 416-443.
Detect Ollama by checking http://localhost:11434/api/tags endpoint. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 300-324.
Detect LM Studio at localhost:1234 endpoint. Fallback after Ollama. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 300-324.
Send commit data to detected LLM for summary and tags generation. Graceful degradation if no LLM available. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 299-327.
Try LLM analysis during background worker, fall back to tracking without analysis if LLM unavailable/slow. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 1035-1044.
Add tests for: background worker, queue management, LLM detection, skip/track decision logic, edge case message parsing. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 766-788.
Create real git repo tests that verify: full commit flow, hook chaining, queue processing, API calls, background worker. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 783-837.
Setup GitHub Actions matrix testing on Ubuntu, macOS, Windows with Node 18.x and 20.x. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 839-865.
Test: multiline messages, emoji in messages, special characters, very long messages, non-ASCII characters, empty messages, binary files, large commits. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 679-707.
Manual testing on all three platforms with real repositories and various commit scenarios.
Write comprehensive help text for all commands with examples. User-friendly error messages.
Write README with: installation, setup guide, usage examples, troubleshooting, configuration options.
Review and improve all error messages for clarity and actionability.
Recruit 5-10 beta testers to use CLI in real workflows. Collect feedback.
Publish commitkit package to npm. Setup npm account, test installation, verify global command works.
Add validation for API token format, add --url flag support, improve success/error messaging. See index.js lines 14-45.
Add mode selection prompt, add connection testing, improve hook installation messaging. See index.js lines 47-92.
Show queue status, worker status, last error, hook status. See index.js lines 94-149.
Implement git history sync: compare git log to API, delete commits not in git. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 23-37.
Display error log with pagination. Add --clear flag to truncate log. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 600.
Show why a specific commit was/wasn't tracked by evaluating all skip rules. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 486-500.
Interactive tool to test ignore rules against hypothetical commits. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 486-500.
Generate default .commitkit-ignore file with common patterns. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 486-500.
Interactive tool to add patterns to .commitkit-ignore file. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 486-500.
Expose worker command for debugging/manual invocation. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 942.
Show if worker is currently running and queue status. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 943.
System diagnostics: check git installation
Remove specific commit from API permanently. Requires confirmation. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 949.
Build Model Context Protocol server for IDE integration. Supports Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 177-287.
Show IDE-specific MCP setup instructions. Add --auto flag for automatic config modification (with user permission). See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 268-287.
Add `commitkit sync --all` to sync entire git history on first setup. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 1052.
Add `commitkit sync --from-date`, `commitkit sync --branch`, selective syncing options.
Generate repository summary from commit patterns. Use for better LLM context. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 236-248.
Implement dead letter queue for permanently failed jobs (after 3 retries with exponential backoff). See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 143-159.
Implement exponential backoff for failed jobs: 2s → 4s → 8s then dead letter. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 143-159.
Add queue cleanup logic to remove old completed jobs and rotate dead letter queue.
Create utilities for reading/writing repo-level .commitkit/config (mode, ignoreFile, created). See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 434-441.
Add timeout handling, retry logic for network failures, better error messages for API errors. See lib/api.js.
Queue commits when offline, automatically sync when connection restored. Worker should detect network availability.
Add test to verify hook script structure: bash shebang, original hook section, CommitKit tracking section, correct ordering. See __tests__/git-hook.test.js lines 88-123.
Add `commitkit analyze --all` to retroactively analyze commits that were tracked without LLM analysis. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 1043.
Create install.sh script that: checks Node version, installs package globally, verifies installation, prints next steps.
Create uninstall.sh that removes hooks from all repos and optionally removes config.
Test on Windows with Git Bash, PowerShell, WSL. Verify bash script execution. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 839-865.
Verify format string parsing correctly handles commit messages with newlines using \x1F separator. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md lines 626-635 and lines 692-697.
Verify that if original user hook exits non-zero, CommitKit skips tracking and propagates exit code. See lib/git-hook.js lines 32-42.
Add GET /api/v1/status endpoint that returns commits count, last commit, account status. For `commitkit status` command.
Add rate limiting to API endpoints: 100 commits/hour per user, 1000 requests/hour per token.
Add pagination to GET /api/v1/commits endpoint: ?page=1&per_page=50. See CLI_ARCHITECTURE_NOTES.md line 247.
Review for security issues: command injection
Test with large repos (10,000+ commits), large queues (100+ pending jobs), rapid commit sequences.
Run worker for extended period with continuous commits to detect memory leaks.
Add COMMITKIT_DEBUG=1 environment variable that enables verbose logging to stdout/stderr.
Add opt-in anonymous telemetry: CLI version, OS, Node version, error rates. For improving product.
Create system for migrating config files when format changes between versions.
Add webhook system: user registers webhook URL, API calls it on commit received.
Add GET /api/v1/commits/search?q=keyword endpoint for searching commit messages and summaries.
If we add tags to commits, create CRUD API: GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/tags, GET /api/v1/commits?tag=feature.
Enhance dashboard UI: filtering by date, search, tags, export to CSV, commit timeline visualization.
Build Chrome/Firefox extension to show CommitKit data on GitHub/GitLab commit pages.
Build VS Code extension with sidebar showing recent commits, inline commit tracking status.
Build iOS/Android app for viewing commit history and generating resume bullets on mobile.
Use LLM to generate professional resume bullet points from clusters of related commits. MVP for post-CLI.
One-click export of resume bullets to LinkedIn experience section (via API if available).
